tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Simple harmonic motion (SHM) occurs when restoring force is proportional to displacement: F = −kx or F = −mg sin θ ≈ −(mg/L)s for small angles. What is oscillating, and what provides the restoring force?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Spring SHM: F = −kx, period T = 2π√(m/k), angular frequency ω = √(k/m). Pendulum (small angle): T = 2π√(L/g). Maximum speed at equilibrium: v_max = Aω. Maximum acceleration at amplitude: a_max = Aω².

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Identify amplitude A (max displacement from equilibrium). At equilibrium, PE is minimum and KE is maximum. At amplitude, v = 0 and energy is all PE. Use energy conservation: ½kA² = ½mv_max² for springs.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Calculate ω = 2π/T or ω = √(k/m). Use x(t) = A cos(ωt + φ) or sin form as given. For energy problems: ½kx² + ½mv² = ½kA² = constant. Solve for unknown position, speed, or time. Check that T has units of seconds.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.

tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Oscillations repeat in a regular cycle. What is one full cycle? What determines how fast the system oscillates — mass, spring constant, length, or gravity?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Frequency f = 1/T. Position in SHM varies sinusoidally. Velocity is maximum at x = 0; acceleration is maximum at x = ±A. Phase constant φ depends on initial conditions (where and which direction at t = 0).

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Distinguish spring-mass from pendulum — different period formulas. For vertical springs, equilibrium stretch is mg/k, but oscillations are still about that equilibrium with T = 2π√(m/k). Don't double-count gravity in the restoring force analysis.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Find k or L from given info. Compute T = 2π√(m/k) or T = 2π√(L/g). If asked for speed at a position: ½mv² = ½k(A² − x²). If asked when object passes a point, use x = A cos(ωt) and solve for t within one period.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.
